title: Punjabi Egg Curry

subs.name: soya chunks (soaked and squeezed) for a vegetarian version; skip the frying step
2 tbsp tomato paste diluted with 150 ml water for a concentrated base
replace half the coriander powder with cumin powder for a slightly different character

steps.text: Score each peeled egg 3–4 times with a sharp knife, cutting just through the white. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai over high flame and fry the eggs, turning gently, until the surface blisters and turns golden in spots — about 3 minutes. Remove and set aside.

Add the remaining oil to the kadai over medium flame. Add cumin seeds and bay leaf; let them crackle. Add the chopped onion and fry until deep golden brown — about 8 minutes. Stir often to prevent burning.

Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook until the raw smell lifts, about 2 minutes. Add the tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook on medium-high heat, pressing them down, until the oil separates — about 7 minutes.
Lower the flame. Add coriander powder, red chilli powder and turmeric; stir for 30 seconds. Pour in 200 ml hot water and bring to a simmer. Taste and adjust salt.
Lower the eggs into the simmering gravy and spoon sauce over them. Simmer on medium-low flame for 5 minutes so the eggs absorb the masala. Add garam masala, stir gently and simmer 2 more minutes.
Taste for salt, garnish with fresh coriander and serve with rice, roti or naan. Halve the eggs at the table so the yolk mixes into the gravy.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → soya chunks (soaked and squeezed) for a vegetarian version; skip the frying step; tomato → 2 tbsp tomato paste diluted with 150 ml water for a concentrated base; coriander-seeds → replace half the coriander powder with cumin powder for a slightly different character
